title.rendered: FieldEdge vs. ServiceTitan (2026): Which Fits Your Business?

content.rendered:

Field Service Software Comparison

FieldEdge vs. ServiceTitan (2026): Which Fits Your Business?

FieldEdge does 80% of what ServiceTitan does at roughly half the cost. The 20% gap matters at scale — but not for mid-size operations.

Research updated: Apr 2026Pricing: $100–$200/user vs. $245–$500+/tech/moBest for: Mid-size HVAC, plumbing, electrical✔ COMPARISON

Visit FieldEdge →

Head-to-Head Comparison

FieldEdge vs ServiceTitan (2026)

FieldEdge
Best for 5 to 20 tech QuickBooks-based shops

VS
ServiceTitan
Best for larger trade businesses that need more depth

FieldEdge is the simpler QuickBooks-first option. ServiceTitan is the bigger bet if you have the staff and budget to absorb it.

These two platforms share the same surface pitch. HVAC, plumbing, electrical, dispatch board, flat-rate pricebook. But they are built for different business sizes, and that difference matters more than any one feature on a sales page.

If you choose wrong, the downside is real. For a smaller shop, ServiceTitan can mean months of setup and a level of cost you did not need. For a bigger operation, FieldEdge can feel too limited once reporting, integrations, and multi-location growth start to matter.

Is FieldEdge owned by ServiceTitan’s competitor?

FieldEdge is owned by Xplor Technologies, a private equity-backed company that also owns other vertical SaaS products. It operates independently from ServiceTitan. The two compete directly in the HVAC, plumbing, and electrical field service space, with FieldEdge targeting the mid-market and ServiceTitan targeting larger enterprises.

Which has better mobile app reviews?

Both hover around 4+ stars in the App Store, but the complaints differ. FieldEdge users tend to report occasional sync delays with QuickBooks. ServiceTitan users more commonly cite app complexity and slow load times on older devices. Check recent reviews (last 3 months) for the most accurate picture — both companies push frequent updates.

Implementation Speed and Complexity

FieldEdge onboarding typically takes 2–4 weeks — significantly faster than ServiceTitan’s 6–12 week implementation cycle. For contractors who need to be running on a new system within a month, FieldEdge reduces the disruption. The training burden is also lighter: most teams are proficient within a week. ServiceTitan’s depth comes at the cost of a steeper learning curve that can frustrate field techs during the transition period.

QuickBooks Integration Depth

FieldEdge’s QuickBooks integration is among the tightest in the industry — it was originally built as a QuickBooks add-on. Two-way sync covers invoices, payments, customer records, and chart of accounts mapping with minimal manual intervention. ServiceTitan connects to QuickBooks too, but contractors frequently report sync issues, duplicate entries, and the need for manual reconciliation. If your bookkeeper lives in QuickBooks, FieldEdge creates less friction.

Cost for Mid-Size Operations

FieldEdge’s pricing is lower than ServiceTitan’s for teams in the 5–20 technician range. While neither publishes pricing transparently, community-reported data suggests FieldEdge runs $100–$200 per user per month versus ServiceTitan’s $245–$500+ per tech. For a 10-tech HVAC shop, that difference can be $15,000–$30,000 per year — real money that could fund a marketing campaign or an additional hire.

Pricebook and Presentation Tools

ServiceTitan’s good-better-best presentation system is the most polished in the industry. Technicians present repair and replacement options on a branded tablet, complete with images, financing options, and instant approval. The result is consistently higher average tickets — contractors regularly report 20–40% increases in revenue per service call after switching. FieldEdge has a pricebook and mobile quoting, but the presentation experience isn’t as consumer-facing or conversion-optimized.

Marketing Attribution

ServiceTitan’s Marketing Pro suite tracks every dollar of ad spend to actual completed revenue — not just leads or booked calls, but jobs that generated income. For operations spending $5,000–$20,000/month on Google Ads, LSA, and direct mail, this visibility transforms marketing from a cost center to a measurable investment. FieldEdge has basic reporting but nothing approaching this level of source-to-revenue attribution.

Membership and Maintenance Agreement Management

ServiceTitan’s membership management tools — automated renewals, revenue forecasting, and recurring visit scheduling — are purpose-built for HVAC and plumbing shops that run 500+ maintenance agreements. If memberships are a strategic revenue pillar for your business, ServiceTitan’s tools are materially better than FieldEdge’s basic recurring job scheduling.

For HVAC, plumbing, and electrical contractors running 5–15 technicians who want strong operational software without the enterprise commitment, FieldEdge is the pragmatic choice. The QuickBooks integration is excellent, the learning curve is manageable, and the cost is reasonable.

For operations running 15+ technicians with $3M+ in revenue, heavy marketing spend, and a need for deep operational analytics, ServiceTitan is the more powerful platform — but it requires the budget, team discipline, and implementation patience to extract value from that power.

The honest answer for most mid-size trade businesses: FieldEdge does 80% of what ServiceTitan does at roughly half the cost. The 20% gap — pricebook presentation, marketing attribution, call analytics — matters at scale but doesn’t justify the premium for smaller operations. For more on each, see our FieldEdge review and ServiceTitan review.

Quick comparison

Feature FieldEdge ServiceTitan
Pricing Quote-based, generally positioned below ServiceTitan Quote-based, widely described as premium-priced
Free trial No No
Best fit 5 to 20 technicians, QuickBooks-centered shops 15+ technicians, growing and multi-location operations
QuickBooks sync Marketed as live, real-time sync Sync available, but real-time depth is less central to the pitch
Integrations Smaller ecosystem Much broader integration library
Built-in financing No Yes
Implementation burden Usually the easier rollout Repeated review complaints about long, difficult onboarding
Reporting and analytics Solid for smaller operations Stronger for larger teams and marketing attribution
Trustpilot signal Lower score, with recurring complaints about updates and GPS Stronger overall review score, despite rollout complaints
Contract risk Reviewers report downgrade and renewal friction Reviewers report contract lock-in and exit difficulty

Where FieldEdge wins

📘

Accounting fit

The QuickBooks connection is a real reason to choose it

FieldEdge leans hard into real-time QuickBooks sync, and actual users cite that as a reason they stay. If QuickBooks is already the financial backbone of the business, that matters more than flashy marketing features.

🛠️

Operational fit

It makes more sense for smaller trade shops

For a 5 to 15 tech HVAC, plumbing, or electrical company, FieldEdge is usually the more practical size match. It is less likely to demand a dedicated internal admin just to keep the system usable.

🚀

Rollout

The implementation risk looks lower

FieldEdge is not complaint-free, but the dominant risk pattern is software instability after updates, not total rollout failure. ServiceTitan reviews describe implementations stretching far beyond the sales timeline, which is a much bigger disruption for a growing business.

👥

Support model

Named onboarding and account support fit owner-led businesses

FieldEdge includes onboarding help and gets repeated praise for specific account managers, even from frustrated customers. If you do not have an operations team, that more hands-on support model can matter.

Request a FieldEdge Demo →

Where ServiceTitan wins

📈

Scale

It is built for businesses that are still getting bigger

ServiceTitan is the stronger fit once you are running a larger team, multiple service managers, or multiple locations. The platform is designed for operational complexity that smaller tools eventually struggle to handle.

🔌

Ecosystem

The integration library is meaningfully broader

If you need marketing tools, financing, fleet systems, reporting layers, and other software connected together, ServiceTitan has a much deeper ecosystem. That matters more as the business gets more operationally complex.

📊

Visibility

Reporting and analytics go further

ServiceTitan is consistently stronger on dashboards, business reporting, and marketing attribution. For a company trying to understand close rates, campaign ROI, and profitability across teams, that depth is the reason to pay more.

💳

Revenue tools

Built-in financing is a real advantage

FieldEdge does not offer the same level of native financing support. If your sales process depends on monthly payment options, ServiceTitan is the more complete platform.

Final verdict

Our call

FieldEdge for most 5 to 20 tech QuickBooks shops. ServiceTitan only if you are large enough to use the extra depth.

Choose FieldEdge if…

FieldEdge

You already live in QuickBooks, your office team is small, and you want a platform that feels more manageable. Go in with open eyes about the review complaints around updates, GPS reliability, and contract friction.

Choose ServiceTitan if…

ServiceTitan

You have the budget, staff capacity, and business size to justify a deeper platform. It earns consideration for larger operations, but only if you are prepared for a heavier implementation and firmer contract negotiation up front.

Request a FieldEdge Demo →
See ServiceTitan Pricing →

FAQ

Is FieldEdge or ServiceTitan better for small HVAC shops?

FieldEdge is usually the better fit for shops with 5 to 20 technicians that run on QuickBooks. It is simpler to implement, generally cheaper, and does not require a dedicated admin to manage. ServiceTitan starts making sense at 15+ techs when you need deeper reporting, broader integrations, and built-in financing. Choosing ServiceTitan too early often means paying for complexity you do not need yet.

Which is cheaper, FieldEdge or ServiceTitan?

Neither publishes public pricing, but FieldEdge is consistently positioned below ServiceTitan in cost. ServiceTitan is widely described as premium-priced with contract lock-in. Both require a sales conversation to get a quote, and neither offers a free trial. Get both quotes in writing with clear renewal terms before making a decision.

Can I switch from FieldEdge to ServiceTitan?

You can, but the transition is not lightweight. ServiceTitan implementations are repeatedly described in reviews as long and difficult, even for new customers starting from scratch. Moving from an existing system adds data migration on top of that. If you are considering the switch, budget for a real implementation timeline and confirm the onboarding process before signing. Also verify your FieldEdge contract terms — reviewers report auto-renewal and downgrade friction.

Does ServiceTitan work with QuickBooks?

ServiceTitan offers QuickBooks sync, but it is not the centerpiece of the platform the way it is with FieldEdge. FieldEdge markets its QuickBooks connection as real-time and bidirectional, and users consistently cite it as the main reason they stay. If QuickBooks is the financial backbone of your business, FieldEdge has the stronger integration story. ServiceTitan’s strength is in broader ecosystem depth and reporting, not accounting sync.

Which has better reporting, FieldEdge or ServiceTitan?

ServiceTitan wins on reporting and analytics. It offers stronger dashboards, marketing attribution, and business intelligence tools designed for larger teams tracking close rates, campaign ROI, and profitability across crews. FieldEdge reporting is solid for smaller operations but does not go as deep. If reporting is a primary decision factor, ServiceTitan is the stronger platform.

excerpt.rendered: FieldEdge and ServiceTitan share the same surface pitch but are built for different companies. FieldEdge works for 5 to 20 tech QuickBooks shops that want a manageable platform. ServiceTitan is for growing operations with dedicated staff who can absorb a real implementation project. Full comparison with user review data.
